digest.top_line: STAMPEDE (M0 high-risk): 2yr abiraterone on ADT+RT cuts MFS HR 0.53 (6yr 82% vs 69%), OS HR 0.60; adding enzalutamide gains nothing.
digest.tldr: Prostate carried the day: 2yr abiraterone added to definitive ADT+RT in high-risk M0 disease (85% RT'd, 74Gy/37fx) drove MFS HR 0.53 and OS HR 0.60. Enzalutamide on top added tox for no extra MFS (HR 1.02). RT-attributable subgroup HR not broken out in source.

sites.intro: The intensification question moves from metastatic into high-risk non-metastatic definitive ADT+RT, where abiraterone (not enzalutamide) carries the benefit.

studies.name: STAMPEDE (abiraterone ± enzalutamide, high-risk non-metastatic)
studies.tldr: MFS HR 0.53 (6yr 82% vs 69%) and OS HR 0.60 adding 2yr abiraterone to ADT in high-risk M0 prostate (85% also had RT).
details: 📊 Efficacy: ADT + abiraterone (±enzalutamide) vs ADT alone
🔍 Pooled meta-analysis of 2 open-label STAMPEDE phase 3 RCTs, N=1974, 113 UK/Swiss sites; median f/u 72mo
🔍 High-risk M0: node+ or (node-neg with ≥2 of T3/T4, Gleason 8-10, PSA≥40); 39% node+, median PSA 34
🔍 RT backbone: 85% (1684/1974) planned RT, 74Gy/37fx to prostate+SV or hypofractionated equivalent
🔍 RT mandated if node-negative, encouraged if node-positive
💊 Abiraterone 1000mg + prednisolone 5mg daily × 2yr on 3yr ADT; control = ADT alone
⚠️ Adding enzalutamide to abiraterone gave no extra MFS benefit (interaction HR 1.02, 0.70-1.50, p=0.91); more tox for no gain
🔗 Extends abiraterone from the metastatic setting into high-risk non-metastatic definitive ADT+RT
⚠️ No RT-treated subgroup HR in source excerpt; benefit presumed across the 85% RT majority, not separately reported

verdict.rationale: Two randomised phase 3 trials pooled; MFS primary hit (HR 0.53) with concordant OS benefit at 72mo, applicable high-risk M0 population. Adding enzalutamide gave no extra benefit.
verdict.audience: High-risk M0 prostate: node+ or ≥2 of T3-4/Gleason 8-10/PSA≥40

studies.significance: The RT read: abiraterone sits on top of definitive ADT+RT (74Gy/37fx to prostate+SV in the 85% who got RT), not RT vs no-RT. It moves systemic intensification for the high-risk M0 pt you're already irradiating; enzalutamide adds nothing over abiraterone (interaction HR 1.02, p=0.91).
studies.monday_clinic: In high-risk M0 prostate (node+, or ≥2 of T3-4/Gleason 8-10/PSA≥40) going to definitive ADT+RT, this supports adding 2yr abiraterone; it does not extend to lower-risk localized disease, and adding enzalutamide buys nothing.

analysis_sections.label: Design
analysis_sections.body: Pooled **meta-analysis** of two open-label phase 3 RCTs within the STAMPEDE platform, 113 UK/Swiss sites, **N=1974**, randomized 1:1. Median follow-up **72 mo** (60-84).

analysis_sections.label: Population & inclusion
analysis_sections.body: High-risk **non-metastatic** disease: node-positive, or if node-negative ≥2 of T3/T4, Gleason 8-10, PSA ≥40; or high-risk relapse. Median age 68, median PSA 34; **39% node-positive**.

analysis_sections.label: Regimen
analysis_sections.body: Abiraterone **1000mg** + prednisolone 5mg daily for **2yr** added to 3yr ADT; the second trial's combination arm also received enzalutamide 160mg. Control = ADT alone.

analysis_sections.label: Radiotherapy
analysis_sections.body: RT planned in **85%** (1684/1974): **74Gy/37fx** to prostate + seminal vesicles or hypofractionated equivalent. **Mandated if node-negative**, encouraged if node-positive.

analysis_sections.label: Endpoints
analysis_sections.body: **Primary: metastasis-free survival.** Secondary: OS, prostate cancer-specific survival, biochemical failure-free survival, PFS, and toxicity.

analysis_sections.label: Results
analysis_sections.body: Adding enzalutamide to abiraterone gave **no extra MFS benefit** (interaction HR 1.02, p=0.91), with no between-trial heterogeneity.

analysis_sections.label: Applies to
analysis_sections.body: Represents high-risk non-metastatic prostate cancer treated with definitive ADT plus radiotherapy. Does not represent metastatic disease, lower-risk localized disease, or patients managed without radiotherapy.

analysis_sections.label: Limitations
analysis_sections.body: Open-label design, though MFS/OS are hard endpoints less prone to ascertainment bias. Pooled across two platform trials; no radiotherapy-treated subgroup HR reported in the source excerpt.

open_questions: Optimal duration of abiraterone (2yr fixed used here)
Whether benefit holds when radiotherapy is omitted
Long-term OS and cure fraction beyond 72 months

related_trials.brief_title: A Study of Metastases Free Survival With Saruparib vs Placebo Added to a Standard RT/ADT in Men With High-risk Prostate Cancer With a BRCA Mutation
related_trials.brief_summary: The purpose of the study is to demonstrate superiority of Saruparib (AZD5305) relative to placebo added to a standard radiation therapy (RT) + androgen deprivation therapy (ADT) regimen by assessment of metastases-free survival in participants with high-risk and very high-risk localised/locally advanced prostate cancer with a breast cancer gene mutation (BRCAm).
related_trials.eligibility_brief:

* Male participants with a histologically documented diagnosis of prostate adenocarcinoma.

related_trials.answers_question: Long-term OS and cure fraction beyond 72 months

related_trials.brief_title: Abi/Pred + ADT vs ADT in PSMA-Positive, Conventionally Node-Negative Prostate Cancer
related_trials.brief_summary:
The advent of PSMA-PET has improved sensitivity and specificity in staging prostate cancer, particularly in intermediate- and high-risk disease. This has created uncertainty in the management of patients with PSMA-positive but conventionally negative pelvic lymphadenopathy (i.e., \<1 cm in smallest diameter).

This study evaluates outcomes of enhanced androgen deprivation therapy (ADT) with abiraterone and prednisone compared to standard ADT, both in combination with radiation therapy, in…
related_trials.eligibility_brief:

papers.title: Abiraterone acetate and prednisolone with or without enzalutamide for high-risk non-metastatic prostate cancer: a meta-analysis of primary results from two randomised controlled phase 3 trials of the STAMPEDE platform protocol
